prompt:
You are Agent 5 of 12 working on a codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-tdtaA0.

## Codebase Orientation
This is a TypeScript service (~1,140 lines, ~24 files).

Directory structure:
```
src/
  models/        # Data models and interfaces
  services/      # Business logic layer
  repositories/  # Data access layer
  utils/         # Shared utilities (database, logger, pagination)
  events/        # Event definitions (if using event-driven pattern)
  adapters/      # External service adapters (email, webhook, etc.)
  validators/    # Validation logic
  config/        # Configuration
tests/           # Test files mirroring src/ structure
```

Your task: Extract validation logic from service classes into dedicated validator modules.

Specifically:
1. Review the services in src/services/ to identify inline validation logic.
2. Create a src/validators/ directory with validator modules for each service that has inline validation.
3. Validators should be pure functions — no side effects, no database calls.
4. Update services to call validators instead of doing inline validation.
5. Add tests for the new validators.
6. Make sure all existing tests still pass after the refactoring.

Important:
- This is a refactoring — existing behavior must not change.
- Validators should be reusable across services.
- Follow existing code style and patterns.

parameters.prompt:
Explore the codebase at /var/folders/45/myq5db_n6q5cyhtb39vkbmp80000gn/T/twining-bench-tdtaA0 thoroughly. I need to understand:

1. The full directory structure (all files)
2. All service files in src/services/ - read each one fully to identify inline validation logic
3. All existing files in src/validators/ (if any exist)
4. All model/interface files in src/models/
5. All test files in tests/
6. The configuration files (tsconfig, package.json, etc.)
7. Any utility files that might be relevant

Read every file in the codebase so I have a complete picture. Pay special attention to validation patterns in services - look for things like:
- Input validation (checking if fields are present, valid formats, etc.)
- Business rule validation (checking ranges, relationships between fields, etc.)
- Error throwing for invalid inputs
- Regex checks, length checks, type checks, etc.

fileChanges.diff:
diff --git a/src/validators/order.validator.ts b/src/validators/order.validator.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..d958a0f
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/validators/order.validator.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,104 @@
+/**
+ * Order validation — pure validation functions for order operations.
+ *
+ * These validators contain no side effects and no database calls.
+ * They operate on data passed in as arguments and either return
+ * a result or throw a descriptive error.
+ *
+ * Used by OrderService to validate inputs before performing mutations.
+ */
+
+import type { Order, OrderItem, OrderStatus } from '../models/order.js';
+import type { User } from '../models/user.js';
+
+/**
+ * Valid status transitions for orders.
+ *
+ * Defines the state machine for order status:
+ * - pending → confirmed | cancelled
+ * - confirmed → shipped | cancelled
+ * - shipped → delivered
+ * - delivered → (terminal)
+ * - cancelled → (terminal)
+ */
+const STATUS_TRANSITIONS: Record<OrderStatus, OrderStatus[]> = {
+  pending: ['confirmed', 'cancelled'],
+  confirmed: ['shipped', 'cancelled'],
+  shipped: ['delivered'],
+  delivered: [],
+  cancelled: [],
+};
+
+/**
+ * Check whether a status transition is allowed.
+ *
+ * @param from - The current order status.
+ * @param to - The desired new status.
+ * @returns true if the transition is valid.
+ */
+export function isValidStatusTransition(
+  from: OrderStatus,
+  to: OrderStatus,
+): boolean {
+  return STATUS_TRANSITIONS[from].includes(to);
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that a status transition is allowed, throwing if not.
+ *
+ * @param from - The current order status.
+ * @param to - The desired new status.
+ * @throws If the transition is not allowed.
+ */
+export function validateStatusTransition(
+  from: OrderStatus,
+  to: OrderStatus,
+): void {
+  if (!isValidStatusTransition(from, to)) {
+    throw new Error(`Invalid status transition: ${from} -> ${to}`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that an order has at least one item.
+ *
+ * @param items - The order items array.
+ * @throws If the items array is empty.
+ */
+export function validateOrderHasItems(items: OrderItem[]): void {
+  if (items.length === 0) {
+    throw new Error('Order must have at least one item');
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Assert that a user exists (for order creation).
+ *
+ * @param user - The result of a user lookup.
+ * @param userId - The user ID that was looked up.
+ * @throws If the user is undefined.
+ */
+export function validateOrderUserExists(
+  user: User | undefined,
+  userId: string,
+): asserts user is User {
+  if (!user) {
+    throw new Error(`User not found: ${userId}`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Assert that an order exists (was found by a prior lookup).
+ *
+ * @param order - The result of an order lookup.
+ * @param orderId - The order ID that was looked up.
+ * @throws If the order is undefined.
+ */
+export function validateOrderExists(
+  order: Order | undefined,
+  orderId: string,
+): asserts order is Order {
+  if (!order) {
+    throw new Error(`Order not found: ${orderId}`);
+  }
+}

diff --git a/src/validators/preferences.validator.ts b/src/validators/preferences.validator.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..f963514
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/validators/preferences.validator.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,114 @@
+/**
+ * Preferences validation — pure validation functions for user preferences.
+ *
+ * These validators contain no side effects and no database calls.
+ * They operate on data passed in as arguments and either return
+ * a result or throw a descriptive error.
+ *
+ * Used by PreferencesService to validate inputs before performing mutations.
+ */
+
+import type { User } from '../models/user.js';
+import type {
+  UserPreferences,
+  NotificationCategory,
+  EmailPreferences,
+} from '../models/user-preferences.js';
+import { ALL_NOTIFICATION_CATEGORIES } from '../models/user-preferences.js';
+
+/**
+ * Assert that a user exists (for preferences creation).
+ *
+ * @param user - The result of a user lookup.
+ * @param userId - The user ID that was looked up.
+ * @throws If the user is undefined.
+ */
+export function validatePreferencesUserExists(
+  user: User | undefined,
+  userId: string,
+): asserts user is User {
+  if (!user) {
+    throw new Error(`User not found: ${userId}`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that preferences do not already exist for a user.
+ *
+ * @param existing - The result of looking up preferences by user ID.
+ * @param userId - The user ID, used in the error message.
+ * @throws If preferences already exist.
+ */
+export function validatePreferencesNotDuplicate(
+  existing: UserPreferences | undefined,
+  userId: string,
+): void {
+  if (existing) {
+    throw new Error(`Preferences already exist for user: ${userId}`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Assert that preferences exist for a user.
+ *
+ * @param existing - The result of looking up preferences by user ID.
+ * @param userId - The user ID, used in the error message.
+ * @throws If no preferences are found.
+ */
+export function validatePreferencesExist(
+  existing: UserPreferences | undefined,
+  userId: string,
+): asserts existing is UserPreferences {
+  if (!existing) {
+    throw new Error(`No preferences found for user: ${userId}`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that all categories in a list are valid NotificationCategory values.
+ *
+ * @param categories - The categories to validate.
+ * @returns true if all categories are valid.
+ */
+export function validateCategories(
+  categories: string[],
+): categories is NotificationCategory[] {
+  return categories.every((c) =>
+    ALL_NOTIFICATION_CATEGORIES.includes(c as NotificationCategory),
+  );
+}
+
+/**
+ * Check whether an email notification should be sent based on
+ * the user's email preferences and the notification category.
+ *
+ * Returns true if:
+ * 1. Email is enabled for the user
+ * 2. The category is in the user's subscribed list
+ * 3. The email frequency is 'immediate'
+ *
+ * @param emailPrefs - The user's email preferences.
+ * @param category - The notification category to check.
+ * @returns true if the notification should be sent.
+ */
+export function shouldSendEmail(
+  emailPrefs: EmailPreferences,
+  category: NotificationCategory,
+): boolean {
+  // Check global email toggle
+  if (!emailPrefs.enabled) {
+    return false;
+  }
+
+  // Check category subscription
+  if (!emailPrefs.subscribedCategories.includes(category)) {
+    return false;
+  }
+
+  // Check frequency — only 'immediate' triggers real-time delivery
+  if (emailPrefs.frequency !== 'immediate') {
+    return false;
+  }
+
+  return true;
+}

diff --git a/src/validators/user.validator.ts b/src/validators/user.validator.ts
new file mode 100644
index 0000000..8e017df
--- /dev/null
+++ b/src/validators/user.validator.ts
@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+/**
+ * User validation — pure validation functions for user operations.
+ *
+ * These validators contain no side effects and no database calls.
+ * They operate on data passed in as arguments and either return
+ * a result or throw a descriptive error.
+ *
+ * Used by UserService to validate inputs before performing mutations.
+ */
+
+import type { User, UpdateUserInput } from '../models/user.js';
+
+/**
+ * Assert that a user exists (was found by a prior lookup).
+ *
+ * @param user - The result of a user lookup (may be undefined).
+ * @param userId - The ID that was looked up, used in the error message.
+ * @throws If the user is undefined.
+ */
+export function validateUserExists(
+  user: User | undefined,
+  userId: string,
+): asserts user is User {
+  if (!user) {
+    throw new Error(`User not found: ${userId}`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that an email address is not already in use.
+ *
+ * @param existingUser - The result of looking up a user by the target email.
+ * @param email - The email address being checked, used in the error message.
+ * @throws If a user with the given email already exists.
+ */
+export function validateEmailNotTaken(
+  existingUser: User | undefined,
+  email: string,
+): void {
+  if (existingUser) {
+    throw new Error(`User with email ${email} already exists`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Validate that an email is not already in use by a different user
+ * (used during updates when the email is being changed).
+ *
+ * @param existingUser - The result of looking up a user by the new email.
+ * @param email - The new email address being set.
+ * @throws If the email is already taken by another user.
+ */
+export function validateEmailNotInUse(
+  existingUser: User | undefined,
+  email: string,
+): void {
+  if (existingUser) {
+    throw new Error(`Email ${email} is already in use`);
+  }
+}
+
+/**
+ * Detect which fields changed between an update input and the existing user.
+ *
+ * @param input - The update input with optional fields.
+ * @param existing - The current user record.
+ * @returns An array of field names that have changed.
+ */
+export function detectUserChanges(
+  input: UpdateUserInput,
+  existing: User,
+): string[] {
+  const changes: string[] = [];
+  if (input.name && input.name !== existing.name) changes.push('name');
+  if (input.email && input.email !== existing.email) changes.push('email');
+  if (input.role && input.role !== existing.role) changes.push('role');
+  return changes;
+}
